notes: Long agentic-session stress: the daemon must keep ONE long, tool-heavy session coherently observed across a LONG multi-turn arc ‚ÄĒ many interleaved assistant-message / tool-call / tool-result iterations ‚ÄĒ with NO spurious flicker to waiting mid-work and a clean FINAL settle to ready when the last turn ends. Recorded over the long-lived run_live path: a leading {"type":"live"} marker forces drive-opencode-interactive.sh onto run_live, which launches ONE opencode TUI under tmux and keeps that SAME process alive across ALL the sequential send/wait_turn turns. This is the deliberate fix for the prior known_failing: the earlier headless recording spawned a fresh `opencode run` per turn, and each subprocess EXITED ~140‚Äď175ms before the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore poller read its terminal `step-finish reason=stop`, so the final working‚Üíready settle landed in the SQLite store AFTER process_exited and was never observed (issue #492 ‚ÄĒ a DRIVER process-exit-before-poll race, NOT a daemon bug; daemon_capability re-verified full on basic-turn/self-correction-iteration where the settle lands BEFORE process_exited). A long-lived process never exits at turn end, so the poller reads the terminal step-finish while the session is still alive and the final settle (`agent finished turn ‚Üí ready`) lands normally ‚ÄĒ the previously-truncated terminal arc is now CAPTURED. Hence known_failing is DROPPED. LOAD-BEARING user-observable signals asserted here: (1) session_birth ‚ÄĒ the ses_‚Ä¶ session appears in ready within 3s of launch, anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; (2) pid_bind ‚ÄĒ the opencode PID binds to the ses_‚Ä¶ session; (3) turn_start ‚ÄĒ the first multi-step prompt drives ready‚Üíworking and establishes the ses_‚Ä¶ identity later phases chain off via same_session_as; (4) final_settle ‚ÄĒ after the FINAL turn the SAME ses_‚Ä¶ session settles working‚Üíready (clean settle, not stuck working), and across the whole arc state NEVER enters waiting (opencode auto-executes its file/list tools in run_live with NO permission gate). final session state is ready. PER-TURN ARC SEPARABILITY IS DE-PINNED on purpose: the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore watcher observes the SQLite store via WAL-write-triggered scans with a 500ms minScanGap debounce (not a per-turn stream), so under run_live's ONE long-lived process the back-to-back turn state-arcs COALESCE into one observed working‚Üíready span (the same transport property proven in token-accounting and mid-turn-message-queued, where adjacent same-ses_ turns collapse under debounce_coalesce) ‚ÄĒ so the spec asserts the SESSION-LEVEL arc (one working span opening at turn_start + the FINAL settle) rather than N separable per-turn arcs, which would over-specify the vehicle through a transport that cannot separate rapid same-session turns. The MULTI-TURN LENGTH and MONOTONIC TOKEN ACCUMULATION across turns are the stress this scenario names but are NOT expressible in the phase DSL (no tool-event form, no TotalTokens/delta metric form): they live in events.jsonl + the replay .golden summary (‚Č•5 tool parts / function_call+function_call_output pairs across the run; cum_input_tokens > 0, cum_output_tokens > 0, non-decreasing across the turn boundaries) and are verified out-of-band at record time. The phase DSL asserts only the session-level STATE arc the stress rides on. Same proven shape as the sibling opencode token-accounting and self-correction-iteration specs.

text: The ses_‚Ä¶ session appears in ready within ~3s of agent launch; never enters waiting (tools auto-execute, no permission gate)
The opencode PID binds to the ses_‚Ä¶ session
The first multi-step file create/read/summarize prompt drives the ses_‚Ä¶ session ready ‚Üí working, opening the long agentic burst

Across all the sequential turns the many write/read/list tool calls auto-execute inside ONE long-lived process with no flicker to waiting, and after the FINAL turn the SAME ses_‚Ä¶ session settles working ‚Üí ready (the previously-truncated terminal settle is now captured under run_live); final session state is ready
